src/ResultStore.ts.summary: Manages in-memory file analysis results for VSCode.

symbols.name: ResultStore
symbols.summary: A class that stores and manages file analysis results in an in-memory map, providing methods to save, retrieve, list all, remove, and clear stored results.

methods.name: save
methods.summary: Saves or overwrites the result for a file by its URI in the internal store.

methods.name: get
methods.summary: Retrieves the last analysis result for a specific file using its URI, returning undefined if no result exists.

methods.name: getAll
methods.summary: Returns all currently stored analysis results as an array, useful for dashboard and status bar display.

methods.name: remove
methods.summary: Deletes the stored result for a specific file when it is closed to prevent stale data accumulation.

methods.name: clear
methods.summary: Removes all stored results from the store, typically called when the user requests to clear all data.

src/reports/UnderstandingGenerator.ts.summary: Generates a structured understanding document for a software project based on its code graph.

symbols.name: UnderstandingGenerator
symbols.summary: A class that generates a structured understanding document of a codebase, including symbols, relationships, and summaries.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes the generator with methods to fetch graph data, summarize files, and interact with an AI scanner.

methods.name: generate
methods.summary: Main method to execute the generation process, reading the code graph, summarizing files, probing the AI, grouping by file, and building the final document.

methods.name: probeAi
methods.summary: Checks if the AI provider is reachable by sending a simple request.

methods.name: groupByFile
methods.summary: Groups nodes (symbols) by their respective file paths for efficient processing.

methods.name: summarizeFileSymbols
methods.summary: Asks the AI for summaries of symbols in each file, falling back to structural descriptions if the AI is unavailable.

methods.name: buildFileEntry
methods.summary: Constructs a detailed entry for each file, including top-level symbols and methods nested under classes.

methods.name: toSymbolEntry
methods.summary: Converts a code node into a symbol entry with its relationships and summary.

methods.name: relationships
methods.summary: Retrieves caller and callee names for a given node, filtering out built-in/global method names.

methods.name: fallbackSummary
methods.summary: Provides a plain description of a symbol when the AI gives no summary.

methods.name: buildGlobalContext
methods.summary: Generates a paragraph summarizing the entire project, falling back to file counts if the AI is unavailable.

src/reports/ProblemsReporter.ts.summary: Generates and displays a code issues report in Markdown and JSON formats.

symbols.name: ProblemsReporter
symbols.summary: A class that generates a report of code issues in a project, including both a human-readable markdown file and a machine-readable JSON file.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes the reporter with a ResultStore for analysis results and a function to get the CodeGraph.

methods.name: generate
methods.summary: Builds the report data, writes both files (markdown and JSON), and opens the markdown file in VSCode.

methods.name: buildReport
methods.summary: Groups issues by file and attaches the enclosing function name using the code graph.

methods.name: enrich
methods.summary: Adds the enclosing function name to an issue based on its line number and the provided code graph.

methods.name: enclosingFunction
methods.summary: Finds the function whose definition is at or before a given line in a file, falling back to '(file scope)' if none precede it.

methods.name: toMarkdown
methods.summary: Converts the report data into a human-readable markdown format grouped by file and function.

methods.name: toJson
methods.summary: Converts the report data into a machine-readable JSON format.

src/publishers/StatusBarManager.ts.summary: Updates the status bar with issue counts and colors based on the result store.

symbols.name: StatusBarManager
symbols.summary: A class that manages the status bar in a VSCode extension, updating it with issue counts and severity levels.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes the StatusBarManager with a reference to an IResultStore and sets up the status bar item.

methods.name: render
methods.summary: Recalculates and updates the status bar text based on the current state of issues in the store.

methods.name: dispose
methods.summary: Disposes of the status bar item when no longer needed.

src/publishers/DiagnosticsPublisher.ts.summary: This file defines a `DiagnosticsPublisher` class that implements the `IResultPresenter` and `vscode.Disposable` interfaces to present code analysis issues as squiggles in the editor's Problems panel.

symbols.name: DiagnosticsPublisher
symbols.summary: A class that implements IResultPresenter and vscode.Disposable to publish diagnostics in the VS Code Problems panel.

methods.name: present
methods.summary: Adds diagnostics for a single file's issues to the Problems panel.

methods.name: clear
methods.summary: Removes diagnostics for a specific file from the Problems panel.

methods.name: clearAll
methods.summary: Clears all diagnostics across all files from the Problems panel.

methods.name: toDiagnostic
methods.summary: Converts an Issue object into a vscode.Diagnostic object suitable for display in the Problems panel.

methods.name: dispose
methods.summary: Disposes of the diagnostic collection, freeing up resources.

src/scanners/ComplexityScanner.ts.summary: Counts cyclomatic complexity in functions and flags those exceeding a threshold.

symbols.name: ComplexityScanner
symbols.summary: A class that scans code for high cyclomatic complexity, implementing the IScanner interface.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes a new instance of ComplexityScanner with a configuration provider.

methods.name: scan
methods.summary: Scans a given document for functions exceeding a complexity threshold and returns issues if found.

methods.name: getAverageComplexity
methods.summary: Calculates the average cyclomatic complexity across all lines in a document.

methods.name: normalizeLang
methods.summary: Normalizes language IDs to a standard format for consistency.

src/scanners/StaticScanner.ts.summary: The `StaticScanner` class scans code documents for issues using language-specific rules.

symbols.name: StaticScanner
symbols.summary: A class implementing IScanner to perform static code analysis on various programming languages.

methods.name: scan
methods.summary: Method that analyzes a given text document and returns an array of issues based on the detected language and rules.

methods.name: getLines
methods.summary: Private method that extracts all lines from a text document as a string array.

src/scanners/AiScanner.ts.summary: The file defines an AI scanner class that integrates with various AI providers to perform code reviews and return parsed issues.

symbols.name: AiScanner
symbols.summary: A class that integrates with various AI providers to scan code for issues and generate text.

methods.name: constructor
methods.summary: Initializes the AiScanner with a configuration provider.

methods.name: scan
methods.summary: Scans a given document using an AI provider, returning parsed issues or an empty array if disabled or too large.

methods.name: generateText
methods.summary: Generates text based on a system prompt and user input using the configured AI provider.

methods.name: callOllama
methods.summary: Calls the Ollama API to analyze code and return issues in JSON format.

methods.name: callHuggingFace
methods.summary: Calls the Hugging Face Inference API for code analysis, handling rate limits and model availability.

methods.name: callOpenAiFormat
methods.summary: Calls APIs that use OpenAI's chat completion format, such as Groq, OpenRouter, LM Studio, and vLLM.

methods.name: callAnthropic
methods.summary: Calls the Anthropic API using its specific message format.

methods.name: parseResponse
methods.summary: Extracts and parses JSON issues from the AI provider's response, handling potential formatting errors.

methods.name: requiresKey
methods.summary: Determines if a key is required for a given AI provider before making an API call.

methods.name: showMissingKeyMessage
methods.summary: Displays a warning message to users about missing API keys and provides options to obtain one or switch providers.

methods.name: handleError
methods.summary: Handles errors from AI provider calls, providing user-friendly messages and guidance.
